title: Tolerate non-canonical refs
author: martinslota
body:
## Summary

Some spec-compliant workbook variants currently throw while being loaded. The goal of this PR is to make those workbooks loadable.

Closes #2591.

## Detailed description

There are two independent fixes, each preceded by a regression-test commit so reviewers can verify both the failure and the fix:
1. [Drawing parts whose root isn't `<xdr:wsDr>`](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/2591). `DrawingXform` returns `null` for unrecognized roots (e.g `<c:userShapes>` chart overlays, or `<wsDr>` declared under the default namespace). We now skip storing those `null`s in `model.drawings` and guard the one consumer that looks drawings up by basename.
1. Worksheet rels pointing at parts loaded under non-canonical paths. Open Packaging Conventions let a workbook name a part anything the rel's `Target` points at, but `exceljs` identifies comments / table / VML drawing parts by filename glob. When a part lives at e.g. `xl/sheet1_comments.xml`, the loader skips it and `WorkSheetXform.reconcile` blows up while walking the rels. We centralize the cleanup as a single `partsByRelType` filter at the top of `reconcile`, so every consumer below can assume its target exists.

## Scope and non-goals

This PR is deliberately a robustness patch, not a redesign. The deeper issues it surfaces are intentionally left for follow-ups:
* Filename-glob dispatch instead of `[Content_Types].xml` routing. The spec-correct fix is to dispatch every part by its declared content type, which would obsolete the regex-based detection for comments, tables, VML drawings, and drawings. That's a much larger and riskier change and is out of scope here.
* Silent data loss. A dropped table / comment / VML drawing currently disappears without a warning. Surfacing those would be a useful follow-up. This PR focuses on preventing crashes on valid inputs.
* Similarly, data for unsupported drawing parts does not get extracted. For instance, `<c:userShapes>` is dropped on the floor rather than rendered; we just stop crashing on it.

## Test plan

Integration tests were added for these fixes (see bb26732ca4edb1311acda9207f33f6bc00ceed18 and 07c43f3ce3e90aa42cc78b06cc28865d9b4bc03e).
